wrapUp: The 48-team World Cup closes tonight with European champions Spain facing defending holders Argentina at New York New Jersey Stadium, in the first final between reigning continental title holders and a clash of generations between 39-year-old Lionel Messi and 19-year-old Lamine Yamal. Saturday's bronze medal match in Miami produced a 6-4 England win over France, the joint fifth-highest-scoring game in World Cup history, as Bukayo Saka's hat-trick secured England's best finish since 1966 and Kylian Mbappe's brace made him the competition's all-time leading scorer with 22 goals. Opta's supercomputer now gives Spain a 59.6 per cent chance of lifting the trophy against an Argentina side who have trailed in three separate knockouts yet always found a way through, while Luis de la Fuente has confirmed Yamal is fit after missing a training session and Facundo Medina remains a calf doubt at left-back. Each story below links to the specific report it is drawn from; squad and fitness details reflect the latest available reporting as of 19 July 2026.

sections.heading: Injuries and fitness doubts
sections.description: Players ruled out, late replacements and day-to-day concerns that could change starting line-ups and squad depth.

stories.headline: De la Fuente confirms Yamal fit for World Cup final
stories.summary: Lamine Yamal sat out Spain's training session in New Jersey on Thursday after picking up a thigh knock in the semi-final win over France, but Luis de la Fuente told reporters on Friday that the teenager is fit to face Argentina. BBC Sport reported the Spain coach said Yamal was back with the squad after precautionary rest, while Al Jazeera's final preview listed no fresh injury concerns in either camp ahead of kick-off at 8pm BST.

stories.headline: Medina races clock for Argentina final selection
stories.summary: Facundo Medina has not played since picking up a calf problem in the round-of-32 win over Cape Verde and remains a doubt for Sunday's final against Spain at New York New Jersey Stadium. The Independent reported the Nice centre-back is in a race against time to be fit, with Nicolas Tagliafico expected to keep the left-back berth if Medina is unavailable.

sections.heading: Squad selection and tactical reshuffles
sections.description: Omissions, late call-ups and manager decisions that reveal how teams plan to navigate the expanded format.

stories.headline: Scaloni weighs De Paul recall for final
stories.summary: Lionel Scaloni is expected to restore Rodrigo De Paul to Argentina's midfield for the final after Giuliano Simeone started the semi-final win over England, with Nahuel Molina also pushing to keep his place at right-back ahead of Gonzalo Montiel. Al Jazeera's predicted XI has De Paul alongside Enzo Fernandez and Alexis Mac Allister behind Lionel Messi and Julian Alvarez in a 4-1-3-2, while Lautaro Martinez remains on the bench despite his stoppage-time semi-final winner.

stories.headline: De la Fuente sticks with semi-final spine for final
stories.summary: Spain head into the final with a largely settled XI, with Rodri and Fabian Ruiz again preferred in midfield over Pedri and Lamine Yamal, Dani Olmo and Alex Baena supporting Mikel Oyarzabal up front. Al Jazeera listed a likely 4-2-3-1 of Simon, Porro, Cubarsi, Laporte, Cucurella, Rodri, Ruiz, Yamal, Olmo, Baena and Oyarzabal, with Nico Williams again expected to begin on the bench after two substitute appearances in the knockouts.

stories.headline: Tuchel rotates heavily as Saka hat-trick seals bronze
stories.summary: Thomas Tuchel made seven changes for England's third-place play-off with France in Miami, leaving Harry Kane and Jude Bellingham on the bench but still seeing Bukayo Saka score a first-half brace and complete his hat-trick from the penalty spot in a 6-4 win. Al Jazeera reported Declan Rice and Ezri Konsa put England 4-0 up before Kylian Mbappe's double sparked a French comeback that Bellingham settled with a stoppage-time solo goal.

sections.heading: Disruption and preparation issues
sections.description: Travel, visa and logistical problems that could affect match readiness rather than just headlines.

stories.headline: Wildfire smoke eases ahead of New Jersey final
stories.summary: Smoke from Canadian wildfires had raised air quality concerns across the north-eastern United States during the build-up week, with several monitors rating New Jersey unhealthy for sensitive groups on Thursday. Al Jazeera reported AccuWeather expects mostly sunny and less humid conditions on Sunday, with the official airnow.gov index forecast to be moderate for kick-off at 3pm local time.

stories.headline: Miami Stadium signs off with 10-goal bronze thriller
stories.summary: England's 6-4 win over France was the highest-scoring game at Miami's Hard Rock Stadium and the joint fifth-highest in World Cup history, drawing a sell-out crowd of 64,478 on the eve of the final in New Jersey. FIFA noted the fixture closed the venue's seven-match World Cup programme at 99.5 per cent capacity, with Kylian Mbappe's two goals taking him past Lionel Messi as the competition's all-time leading scorer.

stories.headline: Finalists converge on New York New Jersey Stadium
stories.summary: Spain and Argentina have had four days to prepare for the 104th match of the tournament at New York New Jersey Stadium on Sunday 19 July, with kick-off at 8pm BST on BBC One and ITV1. Al Jazeera noted the venue hosted Norway's shock win over Brazil in the round of 16 and will stage the first World Cup final between reigning European and South American champions, with Slovenian referee Slavko Vincic appointed.

sections.heading: Analysis and tournament outlook
sections.description: Deeper previews on favourites, group dynamics and how the 48-team format changes the path to the final.

stories.headline: Messi targets history in likely last World Cup final
stories.summary: Lionel Messi will become the oldest outfield player to start a World Cup final and only the second after Cafu to appear in three, with eight goals and four assists at this tournament putting him in the Golden Boot race before Mbappe's bronze brace. Al Jazeera reported Scaloni called Messi's run at 39 incredible and noted Argentina could become the first team since Brazil in 1962 to retain the trophy, facing the country where Messi spent most of his club career.

stories.headline: Opta makes Spain favourites for Sunday final
stories.summary: Opta Analyst reported Spain carry a 59.6 per cent probability of winning the World Cup after 25,000 supercomputer simulations, with Argentina at 40.4 per cent. Inside 90 minutes, Spain were projected to win 45.0 per cent of simulations, with a 29.0 per cent chance of a draw and Argentina winning in regulation time 26.0 per cent of the time.

stories.headline: Mbappe breaks World Cup scoring record in bronze match
stories.summary: Kylian Mbappe scored twice in France's 6-4 defeat to England in Miami to become the all-time leading scorer in World Cup history with 22 goals, moving one clear of Lionel Messi and also securing the 2026 Golden Boot with 10 tournament goals. FIFA confirmed Mbappe is only the third man to reach double figures at a single World Cup since Gerd Muller in 1970, though Messi could still match his eight goals with a hat-trick in the final.

stories.headline: Best attack meets best defence in final
stories.summary: BBC Sport analysis noted Argentina have scored 19 goals at the tournament, the most of any side, while Spain have conceded only one and hold the lowest defensive expected-goals figure since 1966. Lamine Yamal and Lionel Messi have attempted 90 of the finalists' combined 209 dribbles, and Spain have won possession in the final third more frequently per 90 minutes than every team except Uruguay.
